title: Finding Lines of Symmetry in a Rectangle

steps.instruction: A line of symmetry divides a shape into two halves that are mirror images of each other.
steps.work: If you imagine folding the shape along the line, both sides would match perfectly.
steps.explanation: Imagine folding the shape in half — if both halves would line up exactly, that fold line is a line of symmetry.

steps.instruction: Imagine folding the rectangle horizontally. Would both halves match?
steps.work: Yes! A horizontal line through the middle divides it into two matching halves.
steps.explanation: The top and bottom halves are the same size and shape.

steps.instruction: Now imagine folding the rectangle vertically. Does it work?
steps.work: Yes! A vertical line through the middle also works.
steps.explanation: The left and right halves are mirror images.

steps.instruction: What about imagining a diagonal fold?
steps.work: No, the two halves do not match when folded along a diagonal (unless it is a square).
steps.explanation: In a non-square rectangle, the diagonal fold creates two triangles that don't overlap perfectly.

steps.instruction: How many lines of symmetry does a rectangle have?
steps.work: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry.
steps.explanation: One horizontal and one vertical — both through the center.

title: Identifying Symmetry in Letters: The Letter H

steps.subgoalLabel: Check for a vertical line of symmetry
steps.instruction: Imagine drawing a vertical line down the middle of H.
steps.work: The left side is a mirror image of the right side. Yes, vertical symmetry!
steps.explanation: The H is the same on the left and right of its center.

steps.subgoalLabel: Check for a horizontal line of symmetry
steps.instruction: Now imagine a horizontal line through the middle of H.
steps.work: The top half mirrors the bottom half. Yes, horizontal symmetry too!
steps.explanation: The H has the crossbar in the middle, making top and bottom identical.

steps.instruction: How many lines of symmetry does the letter H have?
steps.work: The letter H has 2 lines of symmetry: one vertical and one horizontal.
steps.explanation: Some shapes and letters can have more than one line of symmetry.
